stageName: Busto de Bureba → Medina de Pomar
tldr: The hilly 126 km stage to Medina de Pomar broke the sprint pattern: a reduced front group went to the line and Mischa Bredewold gave SD Worx-Protime a third stage win, ahead of Mireia Benito and her leader Lorena Wiebes. Wiebes held the purple jersey into the queen stage, with Bredewold and Benito her closest GC rivals.

narrative.headline: Bredewold completes the SD Worx hat-trick on the hilly day
narrative.body:
Stage 3's lumpier 126 km from Busto de Bureba to Medina de Pomar broke the pure-sprint pattern of the opening days. The climbs along the route thinned the bunch and a reduced front group contested the finish, with SD Worx-Protime again on the front — this time playing a different card.

Mischa Bredewold went clear in the closing kilometres and held on for the win over Mireia Benito (AG Insurance-Soudal), giving SD Worx a third straight stage and a second different winner. Lorena Wiebes took third on the stage at +11" to protect her overall lead, with Ashleigh Moolman-Pasio, Usoa Ostolaza, Cédrine Kerbaol, Shirin van Anrooij and Évita Muzic all in the lead group. The result kept Wiebes in purple but tightened the GC just below her — Bredewold and Benito had moved within a handful of seconds — heading into the queen stage at Lagunas de Neila that fell outside this calendar's coverage.

gcImpact.headline: Wiebes holds purple; GC tightens beneath her
gcImpact.body: After Stage 3 Wiebes still led the general classification, but the gaps below her had compressed on the hilly day. Bredewold sat second at +3", Mireia Benito third at +7", then a cluster at +25" headed by Cédrine Kerbaol, Usoa Ostolaza, Caroline Andersson, Shirin van Anrooij, Yara Kastelijn, Amanda Spratt and Maud Oudeman. The standings going into the queen stage were a sprinter-led GC about to be overturned by the climbers on the Lagunas de Neila summit finish (Stage 4, not covered here).

riderPerformances.name: Mischa Bredewold
riderPerformances.narrative: SD Worx's puncheur came good on the hilly day, attacking late and holding off Mireia Benito to win in Medina de Pomar. It was the team's third consecutive stage and gave them a second winner alongside Wiebes, while also moving Bredewold up to second on GC behind her leader.

keyMoments.what: Late move to win from the reduced front group

riderPerformances.name: Lorena Wiebes
riderPerformances.narrative: Surviving the climbs to take third on a stage that wasn't built for her was the day's quiet headline — it protected the purple jersey through the last day before the mountains. With teammate Bredewold winning ahead of her, SD Worx controlled both the stage and the GC.

keyMoments.what: Held the lead group on the hilly stage to retain the jersey

riderPerformances.name: Évita Muzic
riderPerformances.narrative: FDJ's GC card came through the hilly stage safely in the lead group, finishing 10th and positioning herself for the summit-finish queen stage where her climbing would count — the day the race would actually be decided.

keyMoments.what: Stayed in the front group, 10th on the stage
